blockingIssues.description: The live HTTP regression test in `test/e2e/test_e2e.ml` compiles a duplicated inline CRUD program instead of the tracked representative fixture `.factory/runtime/interop-crud.rg`, so automated coverage can pass while the real validation fixture drifts.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented `.factory/runtime/interop-crud.rg` as the canonical representative CRUD validation fixture in `.factory/library/environment.md` so future automation reuses the tracked live-validation source.

Documented the dune hidden-directory requirement in `.factory/library/environment.md`: tracked fixtures under `.factory/` require root opt-in via `(dirs :standard .factory)` plus explicit test `deps`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether pure regression-test features are exempt from the compiler-worker manual HTTP exercise requirement, or require workers to report a deviation when they skip the compile/build/run/curl command capture mandated for HTTP-facing work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviewer for `misc-http-crud-runtime-regression-coverage` found that `.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md` still requires manual `127.0.0.1:3111` exercise and exact command capture for HTTP-facing work, but worker session `300a0c87-428f-48ef-a3ab-dbd9a059cb1e` recorded only repo validator commands while marking `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.
